source.title: A beautiful loop: An active inference theory of consciousness

confidence_rationale: Recent (2025) Friston paper proposing a 'hyper-model for precision-control, whose latent states (or parameters) encode and control the overall structure and weighting rules for all layers of inference.' Directly relevant to the MECH-204 question because the hyper-model is exactly the architectural shape Phase 7 / Option B would instantiate. Source quality moderate-high (Neurosci Biobehav Rev; Friston co-author). Mapping fidelity moderate: the hyper-model proposal is read as a SEPARATE precision-control system that broadcasts globally integrated preferences for precision -- this is closer to F3 / Option B than F1's passive parameter consumption. So this paper TILTS the reading toward F3, against the Hobson-Hong-Friston 2014 + Walker-Stickgold 2006 F1-sufficient reading. Mixed direction acknowledged because the paper supports the 'biology runs both' dual-arm reading from Q-042 rather than cleanly supporting F1 OR F3 alone.
failure_signatures: If the hyper-model framework turns out to be specifically about waking conscious-experience integration rather than about REM-driven precision recalibration -- i.e. the precision-control hyper-model operates only during waking, not as a consumer of REM-captured zero-points -- the F3-tilting reading would weaken and the paper would collapse to F1-supporting via the broader Friston framework.

mapping.source_claim_statement: We propose a hyper-model for precision-control, whose latent states (or parameters) encode and control the overall structure and weighting rules for all layers of inference. These globally integrated preferences for precision enact the epistemic agency and flexibility reminiscent of general intelligence. Bayesian beliefs about precision are recursively shared throughout the system in a hierarchical loop.
mapping.ree_translation: The hyper-model for precision-control is the active-inference framing of REE's Phase 7 / Option B (broadcast read at action selection): a separate global precision-control system that DOES consume precision parameters live, broadcasting them through the inference hierarchy at choice time. This is structurally distinct from F1's passive parameter consumption (where waking just runs on the refined model parameters without a separate broadcast channel). Laukkonen-Friston-Chandaria 2025 supports the existence of an explicit precision-broadcast system in biology; if mapped to REE this would license Phase 7 / Option B as the implementation of the hyper-model layer for the REM-recalibrated reference. The mixed-direction designation reflects that this paper TILTS the F1-vs-F3 reading toward F3 / Option B but does not falsify F1 -- biology may run both, with the hyper-model as the broadcast arm and the cumulative parameter refinement as the F1 arm (the dual-arm pattern Q-042 already established for general waking precision-update timing).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper proposes the hyper-model in the context of CONSCIOUSNESS more than REM-driven recalibration specifically. The mapping from 'globally integrated preferences for precision' to REE's Phase 7 broadcast read of precision_at_rem_entry is plausible but one architectural step removed -- the paper does not specifically address whether the hyper-model consumes REM-captured zero-points or operates only on within-waking precision dynamics. Lower confidence than the 2014 Hobson-Hong-Friston paper because the architectural fit to MECH-204 is more inferential.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical / philosophical review in Neuroscience and Biobehavioral Reviews. Recent (2025) Friston paper. No new empirical data; proposes a theoretical hyper-model architecture.

confidence_components.notes: Neurosci Biobehav Rev is high-quality; Friston co-authorship strong. Mapping fidelity moderate -- the hyper-model is proposed for consciousness more than REM-specific recalibration; whether it consumes REM-captured zero-points is inferential. Transfer risk moderate because the hyper-model pattern is theoretical rather than empirically established. Mixed direction reflects that this paper tilts toward F3 / Option B but does not falsify F1, leaving the dual-arm reading as the most defensible synthesis.
